entries.speaker: tash
lines: Sable. You still owe me for that Ashport job.

entries.speaker: sable
lines:
I owe a lot of people. You're near the top of a very
long list.

entries.speaker: tash
lines:
Your engineer friend is in the upper city. Hidden
workshop off the tertiary canal. She's been there two weeks, nose
buried in stolen documents. Doesn't eat much.

entries.speaker: sable
lines: That sounds like Lira.

entries.speaker: tash
lines:
I got you papers. Forged Compact engineering credentials.
Good enough for checkpoints, not good enough for a thorough search.
Don't get thoroughly searched.

entries.speaker: lira
lines: Close the door. I'm close.

entries.speaker: sable
lines: Close to what?

entries.speaker: lira
lines: Sable. You found me.

entries.speaker: sable
lines:
I found everyone. That's my thing now, apparently.
Finding people who don't want to be found.

entries.speaker: lira
lines:
I want to be found. I just didn't want to stop working
long enough to be findable.

entries.speaker: lira
lines:
Cael's trail. He's not hiding — he went straight to
the Convergence. The Compact has surveillance records. Three
separate patrols spotted him crossing the Wastes. He's building
something at the center of it.

entries.speaker: edren
lines: How long ago?

entries.speaker: lira
lines: Weeks. He's had time.

entries.speaker: torren
lines:
The spirits won't go near the Convergence. Whatever
he's building, it's drawing the Pallor to it.

entries.speaker: lira
lines:
There's a problem. The map to the Convergence — the
detailed route, the passes through the Wastes — it's locked in
Ironmark Citadel. Kole has it.

entries.speaker: sable
lines: Kole. The general?

entries.speaker: lira
lines:
The general who decided that if the ley lines won't
cooperate, Pallor energy makes a fine substitute. He's running
the Citadel on it. His soldiers are... different now.

entries.speaker: brant
lines:
I didn't open that gate for you. I opened it for me.
There's a difference. Don't thank me.

entries.speaker: sable
lines: Wasn't planning on it.

entries.speaker: brant
lines:
Kole's on the command level. The soldiers between you
and him — they're not soldiers anymore. They follow orders because
the Pallor energy strips out everything that would make them
question.

entries.speaker: brant
lines: I'll be here when it's done. Or I won't. Either way.

entries.speaker: veld
lines:
He said it was a choice. It was. I chose no. Then I
learned what "no" costs in the new Compact.

entries.speaker: veld
lines:
There were thirty of us who refused. I count sixteen
now. The others didn't die. They just stopped being themselves.

entries.speaker: sable
lines: We're taking down Kole. What can you tell us?

entries.speaker: veld
lines:
His command chamber is on the top level. The Pallor
energy flows from a conduit he installed — shut that down and his
hold on the soldiers breaks. The map you want is in the war room,
one floor below.

entries.speaker: kole
lines:
You think I was corrupted? I was clarified. The ley
lines failed. The Consortium failed. The treaties, the alliances,
the careful politics — all of it failed. The only thing that
doesn't fail is certainty. And the Pallor is very, very certain.

entries.speaker: lira
lines: Certainty isn't the same as truth.

entries.speaker: kole
lines: No. It's better. Truth changes. Certainty endures.

entries.speaker: lira
lines: This is it. The route to Cael.

entries.speaker: lira
lines: He went alone. Through all of this. Alone.

entries.speaker: edren
lines: He's not alone anymore.
(If Edren NOT found.)

entries.speaker: sable
lines: Neither are we. Come on. More people to find.
